seo.title: Connecticut Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in Connecticut — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license connecticut, dental assistant certification connecticut, RDA connecticut, DANB CDA connecticut, dental assistant radiography connecticut

board.name: Connecticut Department of Public Health
board.mailing_address: 410 Capitol Ave., MS #12HLS, P.O. Box 340308, Hartford, CT 06134-0308

license_types.title: Dental Assistant (no state license required)
license_types.description: No state-level credential required for general dental assistants in Connecticut.

steps.title: Meet Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: Ensure you are at least 18 years old and have a high school diploma or equivalent. You must also satisfy one of the following: 1) Complete a DPH-approved course in dental radiography (minimum 36 hours classroom and clinical), OR 2) Have at least six months of clinical experience in a dental office and complete a program of study in radiation health and safety and radiographic techniques, attested to by a licensed dentist.

steps.title: Access the eLicense Portal
steps.description: Navigate to the Connecticut eLicense portal at https://www.elicense.ct.gov/ and create an account if you do not already have one. This is where you will submit your application and supporting documents for Dental Radiographer certification.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your eLicense account, select 'Dental Professions', and then locate the 'Dental Radiographer' application. Fill out all required sections accurately and completely, providing personal, educational, and experience details.

steps.title: Upload Required Documentation
steps.description: Submit scanned copies of your official high school diploma/GED, proof of age, and either: the official transcript/certificate for your DPH-approved radiography course, OR a completed affidavit signed by a licensed dentist attesting to your training and experience. Applicants holding a current DANB Dental Radiography Certificate are exempt from these specific educational/experience requirements.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $105 through the eLicense portal using a credit card or electronic check. Your application will not be processed until the fee is received.

steps.title: Await Department Review
steps.description: The Connecticut Department of Public Health will review your submitted application and all supporting documentation. You will be notified via the eLicense portal or email regarding the status of your application and any further steps required.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Connecticut does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. DANB national certification is recognized and recommended.

faqs.question: Does Connecticut require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: No, Connecticut does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Connecticut?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Connecticut.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Connecticut?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Connecticut?
faqs.answer: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in Connecticut. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.

reciprocity.notes: Connecticut does not issue a general dental assistant license. Radiography competency must be demonstrated.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Connecticut
hero.dek: Please note that Connecticut does not license general Dental Assistants. To become certified as a Dental Radiographer (a regulated function for dental assistants) in Connecticut, applicants must apply through the state's eLicense online system, administered by the Department of Public Health.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the eLicense portal, click 'Register for a new account' and follow prompts to create an account with a valid email address and password. Once registered, log in to access application forms.
